description.task: Given a list of activities with start and end times, select the maximum number of non-overlapping activities. Use the greedy strategy: always select the activity that ends earliest and is compatible with the last selected activity.
instructions: Greedy proves optimal here: choosing earliest-ending activity maximizes remaining time

description.task: Solve the Meeting Rooms problem: given a list of meeting intervals, find the minimum number of meeting rooms required so all meetings can happen simultaneously. Use a priority queue (min-heap) to track meeting end times.
instructions: endTimes.add(intervals[i][1]); // assign this meeting to a room
m1={[0,30],[5,10],[15,20]}: [5,10] conflicts with [0,30]; [15,20] doesn't conflict with [5,10] but conflicts with [0,30] → 2 rooms

description.task: Given a list of intervals, find the minimum number of intervals to remove so the remaining intervals are non-overlapping. Use the greedy approach: sort by end time and greedily keep intervals that don't overlap with the last kept one.
instructions: minRemovals = total intervals - max non-overlapping intervals
